steps.title: Set up the product
steps.content: The function computes the product of an input number and a number that is 74 more than that input. If the input is $x$, then: $$f(x) = x(x + 74)$$

steps.title: Expand to find the function equation
steps.content: Apply the distributive property: $$f(x) = x(x + 74) = x^2 + 74x$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $f(x)=x^{2}+x+74$: The $+x$ term has no origin in the problem; this doesn't come from $x(x+74)$. ✗
  • Choice B — $f(x)=x^{2}+74$: Missing the $74x$ cross-term; this would be $x^2 + 74$, not a product of $x$ and $x+74$. ✗
  • Choice C — $f(x)=x^{2}+74x$: Exactly $x(x+74)$ expanded, the product of the number and the number 74 more than it. ✓
  • Choice D — $f(x)=x^{2}+74x+74$: The extra $+74$ constant doesn't come from the multiplication. ✗
That is the correct answer.
